title: Too many open files read_parquet with LIMIT
description:
### What happens?

When trying to use `read_parquet` with no `LIMIT` clause attached to read a directory full of a bunch of files it works normally, however if I include a `LIMIT` I instead get the error:
`Cannot open file "<filename here>" :  Too many open files`

Interestingly I only see this when the LIMIT is within a certain range, it must be greater than 50 and less than or equal to 1_000_000. 

### To Reproduce

Create dataset:
```sql
CALL dbgen(sf=10);
COPY (SELECT *, strftime(l_shipdate, '%Y-%m') AS l_ship_month FROM lineitem)
TO 'lineitem.parquet'
(FORMAT 'parquet', PARTITION_BY (l_ship_month, l_shipmode));
```

Try and read:
```sql
FROM 'lineitem.parquet' LIMIT 50; -- Works
FROM 'lineitem.parquet' LIMIT 51; --Fails
FROM 'lineitem.parquet' LIMIT 1000000; -- Fails
FROM 'lineitem.parquet' LIMIT 1000001; -- Works
```

Copying to a parquet also seems to cause the error

### OS:

Linux x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

v1.4.0-dev3597

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_
